commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]

target.title: fix(discord/security-audit): include top-level allowFrom in ownerAllowFromConfigured
target.body:
## Summary

The `channels.discord.commands.native.no_allowlists` audit warning is supposed to fire when a Discord operator hasn't configured any allowlist that would let users invoke `/…` slash commands. Today it only consults legacy `channels.discord.dm.allowFrom` plus the credential store when computing `ownerAllowFromConfigured`, even though:

- the audit's own remediation text tells users to add their id to `channels.discord.allowFrom` (the documented preferred path), and
- runtime authorization in `extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.ts` already honors top-level `allowFrom` via `discordConfig?.allowFrom ?? discordConfig?.dm?.allowFrom ?? []`.

So a user who follows the audit's own remediation sees the warning persist no matter where they put their id.

This PR makes the audit match runtime: `ownerAllowFromConfigured` now also includes top-level `discordCfg.allowFrom`, normalized through `normalizeAllowFromList` like the existing inputs.

Fixes #39341. Earlier triage at #67222 (closed as duplicate by `clawsweeper`).

## Changes

- `extensions/discord/src/security-audit.ts` — read `discordCfg.allowFrom` alongside `dm.allowFrom` and the credential store; pass all three through `normalizeAllowFromList`.
- `extensions/discord/src/security-audit.test.ts` — add a parameterized regression case asserting that a top-level `allowFrom` snowflake suppresses the warning, alongside the existing `dm.allowFrom` case.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — entry under `Unreleased → Fixes`.

Account-scoped paths (`channels.discord.accounts.<id>.allowFrom`) flow through the same code path because `params.account.config` already resolves to the account's config object before this function runs; no separate handling needed.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test extensions/discord/src/security-audit.test.ts` — 8/8 pass (was 7; +1 new regression case).
- [x] `pnpm check:changed` — typecheck + lint + import-cycles all green for changed scope.
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1` on touched files — clean.

## Not in scope

- The `dangerouslyAllowNameMatching` / name-based-entries check at `:108-129` already reads top-level `allowFrom` (line 64) — only the `ownerAllowFromConfigured` calculation downstream was missing it, so this is a one-spot fix.
- No remediation-text changes; the existing text was already correct, the audit's *check* was the part out of sync.
target.author: chrispatil
